physiologicalParams.name: Growth & bone development
physiologicalParams.typical: Steady height gain of about 5–6 cm per year at this age
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: On-track growth with strong bone mineralization
physiologicalParams.driver: Adequate calcium, vitamin D, and protein plus running and jumping play

physiologicalParams.name: Motor skills & coordination
physiologicalParams.typical: Refining balance, throwing, catching, and climbing skills
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Noticeably smoother, more confident movement over 8–12 weeks
physiologicalParams.driver: Varied play — ball sports, swimming, playground climbing

physiologicalParams.name: Aerobic fitness
physiologicalParams.typical: Can sustain 20–30 minutes of continuous active play
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Longer play stamina and quicker recovery between games
physiologicalParams.driver: 45–75 minutes of moderate-to-vigorous play most days

physiologicalParams.name: Healthy weight trajectory (BMI-for-age)
physiologicalParams.typical: BMI around 16.4 sits mid-range on the healthy percentile band
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Continues growing along the same healthy percentile curve
physiologicalParams.driver: Energy intake matched to growth and daily activity — no dieting

physiologicalParams.name: Muscular strength & posture
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Better climbing, jumping, and sitting posture in 6–10 weeks
physiologicalParams.driver: Monkey bars, swimming, and ball games load muscles naturally

physiologicalParams.name: Sleep duration & quality
physiologicalParams.typical: 9–12 hours per night recommended for ages 6–12
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Faster sleep onset and fewer night wakings
physiologicalParams.driver: Daytime outdoor activity and a consistent meal rhythm

physiologicalParams.name: Focus & mood
physiologicalParams.typical: Attention dips when meals are skipped or sugary
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: Steadier attention and mood through the school day
physiologicalParams.driver: Regular meals combining protein, fiber, and slow carbohydrates

physiologicalParams.name: Digestive regularity
physiologicalParams.expectedChange: More regular with less constipation within 1–2 weeks
physiologicalParams.driver: 15–19 g of daily fiber plus water and active play

meals.items: Oatmeal with banana slices and a spoonful of peanut butter; low-fat milk
Grilled cheese on whole wheat, tomato soup, apple slices
Apple-cinnamon rice cakes with a thin spread of cream cheese
Baked chicken tenders, corn on the cob, roasted green beans, small dinner roll

week.highlight: Steady breakfast carbs and a familiar comfort-food lunch fuel an active school day capped by soccer.

meals.items: Whole-grain cereal with low-fat milk and sliced strawberries
Small chicken quesadilla with mild salsa, cucumber sticks, mango chunks
Guacamole with baked tortilla chips
Soft bean-and-cheese tacos with lettuce and tomato, cilantro-lime rice; low-fat milk

exercise.activity: Family bike ride around the neighborhood

week.highlight: Latin flavors make beans and avocado fun — an easy fiber win before an evening family bike ride.

meals.items: Whole-wheat toast with almond butter and banana; low-fat milk
Vegetable chicken fried rice with edamame; an orange
Rice crackers with a cheese wedge
Teriyaki salmon with steamed rice and stir-fried broccoli and carrots

exercise.activity: Playground games — tag, climbing, monkey bars

week.highlight: An Asian-inspired day where colorful stir-fried vegetables ride along with a kid favorite, teriyaki salmon.

meals.items: Greek yogurt parfait with honey, berries, and granola
Whole-wheat pita with hummus, turkey, and cucumber; grapes
Apple slices with a small handful of almonds
Baked lemon chicken with orzo, roasted zucchini and cherry tomatoes; low-fat milk

exercise.activity: Swimming lesson plus free splash time

week.highlight: Mediterranean staples — yogurt, hummus, olive oil — keep energy smooth through an afternoon swim lesson.

meals.items: Banana-oat pancakes with a little maple syrup; low-fat milk
Ham and cheese roll-ups, whole-grain crackers, cherry tomatoes, a peach
Air-popped popcorn and a clementine
Homemade veggie pizza with peppers and mushrooms, side salad; low-fat milk

exercise.activity: Easy family walk and light stretching

week.highlight: Pizza night done at home — same fun, more vegetables — lands on the week's lighter recovery day.

meals.items: Whole-wheat vegetable paratha with plain yogurt; mango slices
Mild chicken curry with basmati rice and cucumber raita
Small mango lassi
Lentil dal with rice, whole-wheat roti, and sautéed spinach

exercise.activity: Basketball at the park and playground time

week.highlight: A South Asian day — dal and roti deliver the week's biggest fiber boost to fuel the longest play session.

meals.items: Veggie omelet with whole-wheat toast; fresh orange juice
Chicken noodle soup with a whole-grain roll; a pear
Banana-yogurt smoothie
Beef and vegetable stew with mashed potatoes; low-fat milk

week.highlight: A slower family day with a warming stew and easy outdoor play before the school week starts again.

micronutrients.name: Iron
micronutrients.keySources: Fortified cereal, beef stew, lentil dal, edamame

micronutrients.name: Vitamin D
micronutrients.keySources: Fortified milk and cereal, salmon, eggs

micronutrients.name: Magnesium
micronutrients.keySources: Whole grains, beans, peanut butter, spinach

micronutrients.name: Zinc
micronutrients.keySources: Beef, chicken, beans, dairy, whole grains

nutrition: Balanced calories support steady growth along the same healthy percentile curve — fuel matched to play, never dieting.
About 1300 mg of daily calcium plus vitamin D from milk, yogurt, and salmon builds bone density during prime growth years.
Regular meals combining protein, fiber, and slow carbs steady blood sugar, which shows up as better classroom focus and mood.
15–19 g of daily fiber from fruit, beans, and whole grains keeps digestion comfortable and regular.
Keeping added sugar under 45 g a day protects developing teeth and trains taste preferences toward real food.
exercise: At least 60 minutes of daily play strengthens the heart and steadily lengthens play stamina.
Jumping, climbing, and running load growing bones exactly when they respond best, building lifelong bone strength.
Varied movement — swimming, ball sports, biking — develops balance, coordination, and throwing skills during the prime motor-learning window.
Active days bring faster sleep onset and deeper sleep, the hours when growth hormone does its main work.
Playing with family and friends builds the lasting habit that active time is fun, not a chore.

notes: For children 6–11 the goal is growth, not weight control: offer regular balanced meals, let appetite guide portion size, and aim for at least 60 minutes of fun, active play every day. Milk with meals and water in between covers hydration and bone-building calcium.
